should_trigger.text: Given a reference URL, clone it into a preview-first GEOFlow template package that preserves existing frontend modules.
Inventory GEOFlow frontend variables and modules, then map homepage, category, article, and archive into a template plan.
Map a reference site's style onto GEOFlow without changing business logic, only the template layer.
Produce a preview-first GEOFlow theme package with tokens, mappings, and activation notes.
Audit the current GEOFlow template and tell me what to optimize on the homepage and article page without touching backend logic.
Do not rebuild the whole theme; adjust the current GEOFlow frontend with incremental design changes and token refinement.
Use a reference site as direction, but mainly optimize the current GEOFlow template with a preview-first redesign pass.

should_not_trigger.text: Use the geoflow CLI to create tasks and publish articles.
Explain the GEOFlow homepage code structure to me.
Modify the database schema so the admin can switch templates.
Build a brand-new marketing website from scratch without GEOFlow module constraints.
Push the new template live right now without preview or confirmation.

near_neighbor.text: Copy the reference site's raw HTML directly into GEOFlow.
Create a skill that manages GEOFlow tasks through the CLI.
Implement backend support in GEOFlow settings so templates can be switched.
Adjust CSS and card hierarchy in the current template, but keep the existing data structure.
